programmes.show_description: ...Ages of British Sculpture. 3/3. Alastair Sooke looks at the 20th century's mixture of innovation, scandal and creativity. Contains some strong language.

programmes.show_name: Canals: The Making of a Nation
programmes.show_description: 6/6. Liz McIvor recounts the story of 'canal mania', the period of activity that helped develop Britain's modern financial economy, now centred in London.
1/4. Julia Bradbury walks alongside the Caledonian Canal in the Scottish Highlands and tells the story of Thomas Telford, who created this engineering marvel.
1/3. Documentary series. A look at how the Normans developed from a band of marauding Vikings into the formidable warriors who conquered England in 1066.

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the Sixties: The Folk Revival
programmes.show_description: 4/10. A trawl through the BBC's archives for 60s music with an acoustic bent. Bob Dylan, Leonard Cohen and Tim Buckley feature.

programmes.show_description: A concert by well-known American folk singers Peter, Paul and Mary, recorded on location at the Southport Theatre. From 1983.
